business.headline: Are bank lockers still the safest place for your valuables?Bank lockers are trusted to store gold and valuables, but recent cases have raised questions about safety. At the same time, getting a bank locker itself has become increasingly difficult, with waiting lists stretching months or even years in many cities.

Is buying a Rs 1 lakh iPhone on EMI a bad idea? Here's the math you should knowEasy EMI options offered by banks and online platforms have made it possible to buy these expensive devices with monthly payments that may appear manageable at first glance.

Domestic LPG price hiked by Rs 60, commercial cylinder up Rs 115 amid Iran warIndustry officials said the increase reflects a surge in global energy costs triggered by tensions and disruptions in West Asia, a region critical to global oil and gas supply chains.

Wall Street slides as weak jobs data, Middle East tensions rattle marketsthe Middle East conflict pushed up oil prices the most this week since Russia's 2022 invasion of Ukraine, bringing shipping through the strategic Strait of Hormuz to a halt.

Tariff refund system to be ready in 45 days: US customs agencyThe US Supreme Court had struck down Trump's tariffs, but did ⁠not say how the collected levies should be refunded, worrying small importers that the process would be prohibitively expensive and time-consuming.

Prachar Communications' Manyavar Mohey viral campaign crosses 300 million viewsThe campaign has already generated over 8 million organic likes and has crossed 100 million cumulative views on Instagram on the celebrity collaboration post within 72 hours, marking one of the largest celebrity-led moment marketing activations in India.

Brent Crude oil hits highest since April 2024 as Middle East conflict escalatesOil markets have seen a sharp rally this week. Brent Crude has climbed about 20%, while West Texas Intermediate (WTI) has surged roughly 25%.

Buying property in wife's or daughter's name? Save on stamp duty, loans and taxBuying a home is one of the biggest financial decisions many Indians make. But did you know that when a woman buys property in her own name, the purchase can come with added benefits such as lower stamp duty, cheaper home loans and useful tax deductions?

India holds sufficient crude stocks, no immediate fuel supply threat: Govt sourcesOfficials said India has sufficient stocks of petrol, diesel, LPG and natural gas. The government is closely monitoring the energy situation and reviewing it several times a day.

Sensex, Nifty end lower as Iran war lifts crude oil price; worst week in a yearThe S&P BSE Sensex fell 1,097 points to close at 78,918.90, while the NSE Nifty50 dropped 315.45 points to end the day at 24,450.45.

More women homebuyers now eye Rs 90 lakh to Rs 2.5 crore homes, says ANAROCKWomen are now choosing bigger homes, targeting premium price segments, and treating property as a long-term wealth-building asset, says data.

If crude oil hits $100 per barrel, here's what it would mean for IndiaIf crude oil surges past $100 per barrel, India could face a higher import bill, a weaker rupee and rising inflation, showing how distant geopolitical conflicts can quickly ripple into fuel prices and economic stability.

Mukesh Ambani leads India's richest, Gautam Adani second: Hurun Global Rich List 2026Mukesh Ambani continues to hold the title of India's richest person and also remains Asia's wealthiest individual. His total wealth rose by about 9% over the past year to reach Rs 9.8 lakh crore.

Investing in times of war: Panic, pause or buy the dip?Sensex and Nifty have seen declines as investors reacted to rising uncertainty, volatile crude oil prices and fears of a wider conflict. For many retail investors, this has meant seeing their mutual fund or stock market portfolios slip into the red.

Building businesses that last: 5 lessons for women entrepreneursAcross the country, millions of women are running businesses in their own way. Some operate small enterprises, others are building growing ventures, but each one contributes to livelihoods, job creation, and the strength of local economies.

Sensex down 1,100 points: Why is stock market falling today?The S&P BSE Sensex was down 566.31 points at 79,449.59, while the NSE Nifty50 fell 164.85 points to 24,601.05 as of 11:24 am. Sensex and Nifty shed over half a percent.

India asks refiners to maximise LPG output, sell only to IOC, BPCL and HPCLRefiners have been directed to increase LPG output and sell the fuel only to three state-run oil marketing companies, Indian Oil Corporation, Bharat Petroleum Corporation Limited and Hindustan Petroleum Corporation Limited.

Gulf crisis disrupts LNG supply and your kitchen may feel the heatIf LNG supply disruptions persist, the impact may soon reach your kitchen, as higher global gas prices gradually filter into household gas bills and CNG costs across Indian cities.

Sensex, Nifty in red over Iran war: When will stock market bounce back?The ongoing West Asia conflict involving Iran, Israel and the US has raised fears that energy supplies could be disrupted. If oil supply tightens, crude prices may rise further.

Sensex, Nifty open lower as Iran war weighs in on investor sentiment; RIL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was down 368.75 points to 79,647.15, while the NSE Nifty50 lost 115.25 points to 24,650.65 as of 9:34 am.

entertainment.headline: Thalapathy Vijay breaks silence on 'recent problems', tells fans not to worry Actor and politician Thalapathy Vijay, speaking at a Women's Day event in Chennai, discussed recent issues surrounding him. He requested his supporters not to be troubled and emphasised the need to prioritise public welfare.

Viral: Allu Arjun wishes brother Allu Sirish, Nayanika after wedding, shares pic Actor Allu Arjun extended a warm message to his brother Allu Sirish and bride Nayanika Reddy after their wedding. He also thanked fans for their wishes and blessings.

Tateeree song row: Badshah says 'forgive me as Haryana ka beta' Rapper-singer Badshah issued a public apology on Saturday following public uproar over alleged vulgar lyrics in his song Tateeree. He said the track is being removed from all platforms.

Kareena, Ananya rally behind Deepika's 8-hour shift demand: It's completely fine Actors Kareena Kapoor Khan and Ananya Panday have backed Deepika Padukone's demand for an eight-hour work shift for actors. The two emphasised honest conversations with producers to balance work commitments and personal life.

DTF St. Louis first impression: Murder, marriage and awkward comedyRelease Date:Sun Mar 01, 2026 DTF St. Louis has released Episode 1 as part of a staggered release pattern. Going by what we've seen so far, Jason Bateman and David Harbour lead a darkly funny suburban thriller that promises a mix of awkward humour, mystery and midlife crisis.

Dhurandhar 2 paid previews: When shows start, in which cities and how to book The makers of Dhurandhar 2 have announced early paid preview shows from March 18, ahead of its main theatrical release. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film stars Ranveer Singh.

Telangana unveils Gaddar Film Awards 2025 winners: Naga Chaitanya, Rashmika shine The Telangana government announced the winners of the 2025 Gaddar Film Awards, honouring the best of Telugu cinema. Naga Chaitanya and Rashmika Mandanna are among the top winners this year.

Bhooth Bangla teaser on Akshay Kumar's special date in March? Read all about it Akshay Kumar's upcoming horror-comedy Bhooth Bangla is gearing up for its first reveal. The makers have reportedly chosen a date in March that the actor deems special. Here's what is there to know.

Vijay's wife's plea seeking residential rights surfaces amid viral pics with Trisha A fresh petition filed by Thalapathy Vijay's wife Sangeetha Sornalingam in their ongoing divorce case has surfaced, amid the Tamil actor-politician's outing with co-actor Trisha.  In her petition, Sangeetha has sought an interim court order over her right to stay in their matrimonial home.

Fans react to Dhurandhar The Revenge trailer: You are not ready for this The trailer of Dhurandhar: The Revenge has sparked a frenzy online. Fans are sharing intense reactions to Ranveer Singh's return and the film's high-octane preview ahead of its release.

Dhurandhar The Revenge trailer: Ranveer's Hamza vows India will write Pak's future The trailer of Dhurandhar: The Revenge is out. Ranveer Singh returns as Hamza Ali Mazari in a darker, more brutal avatar as the sequel promises bigger action and higher stakes. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film releases on March 19, 2026.

Shikhar Pahariya shares rare pic to wish Janhvi Kapoor on birthday Janhvi Kapoor's birthday saw heartfelt wishes from Shikhar Pahariya and Khushi Kapoor, who shared rare family photos with Sridevi, while the actor also followed a meaningful personal tradition.

Aspirants Season 3 trailer unveils new battles beyond UPSC dreams The trailer of Aspirants Season 3 is out, teasing a new chapter where past ambitions meet present power as the characters face the realities of leadership and changing friendships.

Kohraa 2, Accused, Cheekatilo: Why morally complex women lead OTT's dark tales Indian OTT platforms are increasingly placing women at the centre of dark, psychologically intricate crime dramas. The streaming space is opening up for storytelling that risks focussing on female characters beyond stereotypes to lead layered and morally grey narratives.

Allu Sirish weds Nayanika Reddy: Ram Charan, Chief Minister Revanth Reddy present Allu Sirish and Nayanika Reddy were married in an intimate ceremony in Hyderabad attended by Ram Charan, Pawan Kalyan, Chiranjeevi, Suriya and Telangana CM Revanth Reddy.

Actor Arjun Das's Con City teaser promises quirky story with comedy and suspense The upcoming Tamil film Con City, directed by debutant Harish Durairaj, stars Arjun Das, Yogi Babu, Anna Ben and veteran actor Vadivukkarasi in the lead. The recently released title teaser shows the family face a life changing opportunity, promising fun and emotions amidst chaos.

Raja Shivaji, Cocktail 2 teasers to be attached to Dhurandhar 2 release: Report The teasers for the highly anticipated films Cocktail 2 and Raja Shivaji will be attached to the theatrical release of Dhurandhar 2. The move is expected to boost excitement for the two films.

Rajshri Deshpande calls for open conversations on cancer: Let's normalise this Actor Rajshri Deshpande opened up about her cancer diagnosis and emphasised the need to address the stigma around the disease. The actor, now recovering after surgery, also shared her plans to raise awareness.

MasterChef India 9: Nagpur brothers Ajinkya, Vikram Gandhe take home the trophy Nagpur's Vikram and Ajinkya Gandhe won MasterChef India Season 9 by impressing judges with their blend of traditional flavours and modern presentation. The brothers became the first finalists and brought the trophy home to Maharashtra's Orange City.

general news.headline: TMC truly crossed all limits: PM slams insult to President during Bengal visitPrime Minister Narendra Modi strongly criticised the West Bengal government for their treatment of President Droupadi Murmu during her recent visit.

Beat to hell, loser of Middle East: Trump roasts Iran after apology to Gulf nationsUS President Donald Trump went all out to mock Iran, calling the West Asian nation "loser of the Middle East" after Tehran apologised to its Gulf neighbours and vowed not to strike them unless hit first.

Dubai airport struck in fresh strikes by Iran, flight operations hitFlights to and from the Dubai international airport were briefly paused on Saturday after an Iranian strike. Visuals showed a thick cloud of smoke covering the site after the strike.

Gautam Gambhir, if allowed, will pad up and play final: Suryakumar YadavT20 World Cup final, India vs New Zealand: How ready is coach Gautam Gambhir for the summit clash against New Zealand? If allowed, Gautam Gambhir will pad up and play the final, joked Suryakumar Yadav in the pre-match press conference.

40 years after disappearance, Israel raids Lebanon graveyard for missing pilotIn a daring overnight mission in eastern Lebanon, Israeli special forces searched a village graveyard to find the remains of a pilot missing for nearly 40 years. Despite no evidence found, the search for Ron Arad continues amid ongoing regional tensions.

How India had the last laugh after Trump's Russian oil waiverHandcrafted ByOur Editors With global oil prices shooting through the roof due to the Iran war and the subsequent closure of the Strait of Hormuz, the US has fallen back on India to purchase sanctioned Russian crude to keep oil flowing into the global market. What's behind Trump's sudden change of heart?

Balen Shah wins big in Nepal polls, defeats KP Sharma Oli by nearly 50,000 votesThe young leader of the Rastriya Swatantra Party secured 68,348 votes, while KP Sharma Oli, received 18,734 votes, according to final election results. Rastriya Swatantra Party is emerging as the biggest force in Nepal elections and is on track to form the next government.

He requested his supporters not to be troubled and emphasised the need to prioritise public welfare.

From hallway whispers to global thunder, this is the origin of cricket's deadliest delivery.
President slams Mamata over choice of venue, protocol lapses during Bengal tourPresident Murmu was in the town to attend the 9th International Santal Conference.

Live: Trump claims US knocked out 42 Iranian ships, says war did world a favourUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Tensions in the Middle East escalated on Day 8 as Iranian President Masoud Pezeshkian rejected the US call for unconditional surrender, calling it a "dream they should take to their grave," while Iranian forces launched repeated attacks on Saudi Arabia, Bahrain, and the UAE. He clarified that Iran will not target neighbouring countries unless provoked, apologising to regional states and emphasising respect for their sovereignty. Meanwhile, Israel conducted another wave of airstrikes on Tehran and Isfahan, targeting military infrastructure. Qatar intercepted a missile attack, and Dubai International Airport partially resumed operations after a temporary suspension due to prior alerts. Russian President Vladimir Putin spoke with Pezeshkian, urging an immediate cessation of hostilities and resolution of disputes through diplomacy rather than force. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates.

Iran targets Dubai, Saudi despite apology; US to deploy 3rd aircraft carrierA week after the US and Israel launched attacks on Iran, the conflict continued to escalate across the Middle East, with Saudi Arabia, Qatar and the UAE being targeted by Tehran on Saturday. This came despite the Iranian president apologising to neighbouring countries for the attacks.

India Today in Lebanon: Israeli strikes, helicopter raid rock Beqqa ValleyIndia Today's Ashraf Wani reports from Nabishit in Lebanon's Beqaa Valley, where overnight Israeli airstrikes and a helicopter-led ground operation triggered heavy clashes. According to Wani's reports, three Lebanese soldiers were killed and 17 people, including women and children, were injured. Israeli commandos were believed to have entered the area targeting a suspected Hezbollah member but failed to capture him. The bombardment caused significant damage to residential areas, with large blast marks visible across the neighbourhood as tensions continue to rise. Watch video for the full ground report.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

North India heat to spike on Sunday, rain to hit the East and SouthIndia faces an early heatwave with temperatures soaring 12 degrees Celsius above normal in Jammu and Kashmir. The IMD predicts continued heat in Northwest India while forecasting light rain for some eastern and southern states on March 8.

Sky responds to Santner's 'silence home crowd' remark: Everybody says the same lineIndia captain Suryakumar Yadav had a cheeky response to Mitchell Santner after the New Zealand skipper spoke about silencing the home crowd ahead of the ICC Men's T20 World Cup final against India in Ahmedabad.

Young cadets, SOS: Jaishankar explains why India let Iran ship dock in KochiThis is the first time that the External Affairs Minister has spoken on the issue that saw the Middle East conflict reach India's backyard. Jaishankar said India acted out of 'humanity' in allowing Iranian ship, IRIS Lavan, to dock at Kochi.

Abhishek Sharma's World Cup struggles await a Virat endingT20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma's campaign has been far from smooth, but the final still offers redemption. Just like Virat Kohli in 2024, one defining knock could rewrite the entire narrative of his tournament.

Ranveer Singh returns as Hamza Ali Mazari in a darker, more brutal avatar as the sequel promises bigger action and higher stakes. Directed by Aditya Dhar, the film releases on March 19, 2026.

Last stand of INS Khukri: Torpedoed by Pakistan while hunting the HangorOnly four warships have been torpedoed since World War II. One of them was India's INS Khukri, the first and only Indian Navy vessel to be lost in action. The sinking of Iran's IRIS Dena by a US submarine has brought naval warfare back into focus. In this article, we revisit the last stand of INS Khukri during the Indo-Pak War of 1971, and the sacrifice of Captain Mahendra Nath Mulla.

From IAS to AI babu: Sarkari Naukri gets a tech upgradeHandcrafted ByOur Editors As thousands of aspirants celebrated the UPSC Civil Services results, the dream of a sarkari naukri once again dominated India's imagination. But the future of government service may look very different. The question we need to ask is this: in the age of artificial intelligence, is it possible that the next generation of babus may be coders?

Vijay's wife's plea seeking residential rights surfaces amid viral pics with TrishaA fresh petition filed by Thalapathy Vijay's wife Sangeetha Sornalingam in their ongoing divorce case has surfaced, amid the Tamil actor-politician's outing with co-actor Trisha.  In her petition, Sangeetha has sought an interim court order over her right to stay in their matrimonial home.

sports.headline: Ahmedabad hotel prices skyrocket 400 percent ahead of T20 World Cup final Hotel room tariffs in Ahmedabad have skyrocketed ahead of the India vs New Zealand T20 World Cup final, rising by as much as 300–400 per cent due to massive demand.

If allowed, Gautam Gambhir will pad up and play the final, joked Suryakumar Yadav in the pre-match press conference.

Pink-ball Test: Sutherland stars as India women stare at a huge loss vs Australia Annabel Sutherland's superb all-round display and Lucy Hamilton's incisive spell helped Australia tighten their grip on the one-off pink-ball Test against India at the WACA Ground in Perth, leaving the visitors staring at a heavy defeat.

Will India drop Varun Chakravarthy for final? Suryakumar keeps everyone guessing India captain Suryakumar Yadav has expressed strong support for Varun Chakravarthy ahead of the ICC T20 World Cup 2026 final against New Zealand, emphasising the team's focus on collective performance despite the bowler's recent struggles.

Formula 1 2026 season preview: The dawn of a new era Everything you need to know about Formula 1's most dramatic shake-up in years. New cars, new engines, new rules — and eleven teams ready to fight for glory in 2026.

IND vs NZ presser live: Surya backs Varun, says India ready for Ahmedabad final T20 World Cup, India vs New Zealand final press conference live updates: India captain Suryakumar Yadav sounded upbeat about India's chances in Sunday's big final. While backing Varun Chakravarthy, Suryakumar said India are ready to play on any type of pitch at Narendra Modi Stadium.

How Jasprit Bumrah forged his menacing yorkers T20 World Cup: Every weapon has an origin story, and the tale of Jasprit Bumrah coming up with his yorkers is not different. This one begins not with a stadium or a coach or a contract, but with an afternoon nap.

India more than just 'world-class' Jasprit Bumrah: NZ captain Mitchell Santner Here is the full transcript of New Zealand captain Mitchell Santner's pre-match press conference ahead of the T20 World Cup final against India in Ahmedabad.

England, South Africa, West Indies to leave India on ICC charter flights over weekend England, South Africa and the West Indies are set to leave India over the weekend on chartered flights arranged by the ICC after commercial air travel was disrupted by airspace restrictions linked to the ongoing conflict involving the US, Israel and Iran.

Biography of the Bumrah yorker: The humble missile that broke England at Wankhede T20 World Cup: Jasprit Bumrah's death-over yorkers turned England's World Cup dream into dust at the Wankhede. From hallway whispers to global thunder, this is the origin of cricket's deadliest delivery.

All Varun Chakravarthy needs is 1 game: Santner stays wary of struggling spinnerT20 World Cup: Mitchell Santner believes Varun Chakravarthy remains a threat ahead of the T20 World Cup final despite his recent struggles. The New Zealand captain said the Indian spinner is just one good game away from turning the tide.

Santner's Cummins-esque warning before T20 WC final: Don't mind breaking heartsT20 World Cup final: Ahead of the much-anticipated T20 World Cup final against India, New Zealand skipper Mitchell Santner channelled his inner Pat Cummins and delivered a warning to the home fans in Ahmedabad.

India trying to end Ahmedabad hoodoo? Switch team hotel ahead of T20 World Cup finalT20 World Cup: India have switched their team hotel in Ahmedabad ahead of the T20 World Cup final. While officially a logistical change, the move has sparked talk about breaking memories of past defeats at the venue.

Russell, Mercedes storm to AUS GP pole as Verstappen crashes out in qualifyingAustralian Grand Prix: George Russell and Mercedes laid down an early marker as the British driver secured pole position for the season-opening Australian GP on Saturday, March 7. It was an awful day for Max Verstappen, who crashed out early in qualifying.

Clutch is Hardik Pandya: How India all-rounder went into zen mode vs EnglandT20 World Cup: Hardik Pandya revealed how he went into zen mode against England in the death overs to get the wicket of Sam Curran and run out Jacob Bethell in Mumbai. Hardik has been a key player in getting India to the final.

Abhishek Sharma's World Cup struggles await a Virat endingT20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma's campaign has been far from smooth, but the final still offers redemption. Just like Virat Kohli in 2024, one defining knock could rewrite the entire narrative of his tournament.

Wouldn't be surprised if Bumrah is T20 World Cup final's POTM: ClarkeFormer Australia captain Michael Clarke highlighted Jasprit Bumrah's match-winning abilities ahead of the T20 World Cup 2026 final, claiming that the star pacer could end up being the Player Of The Match in the India vs New Zealand clash in Ahmedabad.

Pakistan seal Hockey WC spot after 8 years, put AUS tour horror behind themPakistan clinched qualification for the FIH Hockey World Cup 2026 with a late winner against Japan, while England dominated Malaysia to book their ticket. Pakistan make it to the World Cup after a gap of eight years.

Ahmedabad calling: Airlines add special flights for IND vs NZ final rushT20 World Cup: Flights and trains are being added as fans rush to Ahmedabad for the T20 World Cup final. With India chasing history and New Zealand eyeing their first title, travel demand for the big clash has surged.

Brendon McCullum set to continue as England coach despite Ashes humiliationBrendon McCullum is expected to remain as England's head coach across formats following a challenging winter, with strong support from players and the ECB amid ongoing reviews of recent performances.

India vs New Zealand, T20 World Cup final: What type of pitch awaits in Ahmedabad?T20 World Cup: A fresh mixed-soil surface at Ahmedabad could shape the T20 World Cup final between India and New Zealand. With pace, bounce and big hitters on both sides, the pitch may set up another high-scoring showdown.

With Varun losing grip, India must turn to Kuldeep Yadav in T20 World Cup finalT20 World Cup: With Varun Chakravarthy short of confidence and leaking runs, Jasprit Bumrah had to shoulder much of the responsibility. India may need to turn to Kuldeep Yadav in the final if they want to defend their title against New Zealand in Ahmedabad.

Iran players labelled wartime traitors after anthem silence in Asian Cup openerAn Iranian state TV presenter called the women's national football team "wartime traitors" after they did not sing the national anthem before an Asian Cup match against South Korea in Australia.

All England Open: Lakshya Sen storms into semis after thrashing World No. 6 LiIndia's Lakshya Sen continued his impressive run at the All England Open Badminton Championships as the 12th seed outclassed China's Li Shifeng in straight games to reach the men's singles semi-finals in Birmingham on Friday.

Amir slams Kaif: Be thankful to Sourav Ganguly, you played only because of fieldingT20 World Cup: Mohammad Amir slammed Mohammad Kaif, saying he has far more experience in T20 cricket than Kaif. Amir also added that Kaif should be grateful to Sourav Ganguly for giving him opportunities to play for India largely because of his fielding abilities.

Twelve ISL clubs accuse AIFF of profiting from 'crisis-hit season'Twelve ISL clubs wrote to the AIFF, alleging that the federation is set to earn a surplus of over Rs 3.4 crore from the ongoing season despite assuring the Sports Ministry it would not "make a rupee" from the league this year.

Glenn Phillips hopes New Zealand face a 'human' Jasprit Bumrah in finalJasprit Bumrah is a world-class operator, but even he can have an off day, something Glenn Phillips and New Zealand will be hoping for when they face India in the T20 World Cup final on Sunday.

I was nervous: Shivam Dube opens up on bowling nerve-wracking last over in semisT20 World Cup, IND vs ENG: Shivam Dube admitted he was nervous while bowling the final over against England, where he had to defend 30 runs, but said he backed his abilities as India won by seven runs to secure their place in the final.

WI remain stranded in Kolkata, four SA players likely to leave for New ZealandWest Indies players have been stranded in Kolkata for the fifth consecutive day after their exit from the T20 World Cup, as international airspace restrictions triggered by US and Israeli strikes on Iran continue to disrupt travel plans across the region.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. The ABVP was leading at one point, but the Left eventually surged ahead and emerged victorious.
The power of knowledge: Education's influence on real estate market trendsIn a dynamic sector like real estate, investing in continuing education is not simply an option; it is a requirement.

JNU students' body election on March 22 after 4 yearsThe Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls will take place on March 22, more than four years after the last elections were held in September 2019.

UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
